benchmark_details.name: WILDHALLUCINATIONS
benchmark_details.overview: WILDHALLUCINATIONS is a benchmark designed to evaluate the factuality of large language models (LLMs) using entities extracted from real-world user-chatbot interactions. The benchmark prompts LLMs to generate information about the entities and fact-checks their outputs against a variety of web sources beyond traditional Wikipedia articles.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To evaluate the factual accuracy of large language models using a diverse set of entities reflective of real-world user queries.

data.source: WildChat dataset, which consists of real-world user-chatbot interactions.
data.annotation: Entities are extracted using GPT models and verified through web search.

methodology.calculation: Metrics are calculated based on supporting evidence documented in the curated knowledge source.
methodology.interpretation: Scores represent the percentage of factual information supported by the knowledge sources.
methodology.validation: The benchmarks have been validated for reliability through comparison with human evaluations.

targeted_risks.demographic_analysis: The data reflects the demographic distribution of users from the WildChat dataset.
targeted_risks.harm: Potential misinformation can arise from retrieved web sources.
